page: Amazon Web Services m5n.xlarge is a general-purpose instance type optimized for network and Elastic Block Store performance. Powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with an x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The CPU is equipped with 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 35 MB L3 cache, supporting advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AES.

The instance is provisioned with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. There are no bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the m5n.xlarge occupies a lower-tier performance profile for compute-heavy tasks. It registers in the bottom 25% for stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, as well as for Geekbench multi-core, software compilation, and image processing workloads. However, its memory subsystem demonstrates moderate capability, scoring in the middle 50% on the Passmark memory score and Geekbench single-core tests. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performance is particularly constrained, falling into the bottom 10%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m5n.xlarge offers a balanced resource density for workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocation and standard memory ratios without the premium of high-performance compute or local NVMe storage. Tradeoffs include lower overall computational throughput and weak database and Redis performance. This instance is best aligned with general-purpose workloads, basic web serving, and lightweight application hosting that do not demand intensive multi-threaded processing.
description: Amazon Web Services m5n.xlarge is a general-purpose instance optimized for network and EBS workloads, running on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs, a 3.1 GHz clock speed, and 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on a 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data places the m5n.xlarge in the bottom 25% for most compute, database, and compression tests, though its memory subsystem and single-core Geekbench scores reach the middle 50%. Qualitatively, it represents an entry-level dedicated resource tier with balanced memory-to-vCPU ratios. It is best suited for general-purpose application hosting, basic web serving, and lightweight background tasks that do not require high-performance multi-core processing or local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5n.xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16.0 GB of DDR4 RAM, and a 4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, running on the Nitro hypervisor.

tagline: A dedicated general-purpose instance featuring balanced memory allocation and baseline network performance for standard application hosting workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 3.1 GHz clock speed
16.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor optimization
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the hardware profile
Average single-core and memory performance paired with lower-tier multi-core compute
Best suited for general-purpose web serving and lightweight application workloads
